issues.description: Action should be 'Rest' but card text says 'Set this Character as active' — this is already active state, not a rest action. The trigger is OnAttack but the effect doesn't match the described game mechanic of using DON to activate. Need clarification: does 'Set as active' mean remove rest status (unrest)? If so, type should reflect that. Current DSL uses 'Rest' which contradicts 'set as active'.

Second effect trigger is 'Activated' but card text specifies '[Activate: Main]' — should be 'Activated' is correct, but the action type 'Rest' again contradicts 'Set this Character as active'. Both effects claim to 'set as active' not 'rest'.
Missing condition: '[Once Per Turn]' should be represented as a condition. First effect should have condition restricting it to once per turn.
Missing condition: '[Once Per Turn]' should be represented as a condition. Second effect should have condition restricting it to once per turn.

suggestedDsl.effectText: [When Attacking] [Once Per Turn] ➀: Set this Character as active. [Activate: Main] [Once Per Turn] ➁: Set this Character as active.

actions.note: DSL lacks 'Unrest' action type — 'set as active' implies removing rest status, not applying it. Recommend clarifying card intent.
Same issue: 'set as active' contradicts 'Rest' action.
